title: Have been vs Had been
subtitle: O mesmo "been", dois pontos de referência: o AGORA ou um momento do passado

blocks.title: Have / Has been
blocks.subtitle: Present Perfect — a situação chega até o AGORA

examples.pt: Estou aqui desde as 8h. (e ainda estou)
Ela tem estado doente esta semana. (semana ainda não acabou)

blocks.title: Had been
blocks.subtitle: Past Perfect — a situação chega até um momento do PASSADO

examples.en: I **had been** there for an hour when she arrived.
examples.pt: Eu estava lá havia uma hora quando ela chegou.

examples.en: He **had been** sick before the trip.
examples.pt: Ele tinha estado doente antes da viagem.

examples.en: They **had been waiting** since noon when the gate opened.
examples.pt: Eles esperavam desde o meio-dia quando o portão abriu.

blocks.title: Comparativo Rápido
rows: `I have been here for an hour.` (ainda estou)
`I had been there for an hour when he came.` (até ele chegar)
há outro verbo no passado por perto

items.wrong: I **have been** waiting for an hour when the bus arrived.
items.right: I **had been** waiting for an hour when the bus arrived.

items.wrong: She **had been** sick since Monday. (e ainda está)
items.note: se continua até agora → has been

items.wrong: He **have been** my friend for years.
items.right: He **has been** my friend for years.

blocks.text: Procure a **âncora** da frase: se existe outro verbo no passado (arrived, called, when I got there…), use **had been**. Se a situação desemboca no agora, use **have/has been**.
Tradução rápida: **have been** ≈ "estou/tenho estado (desde/há)"; **had been** ≈ "estava/tinha estado (quando algo aconteceu)".

exercises.question: I ___ in this company for five years — and I still love it.
exercises.explanation: A situação continua até **agora** ("still love it") → Present Perfect: **have been**.

exercises.question: She ___ at the party for two hours when he finally arrived.
exercises.explanation: "Arrived" ancora a frase no **passado** → **had been** (até aquele momento).

exercises.question: He ___ sick since Tuesday, so he's still at home today.
exercises.explanation: Continua até hoje ("still at home") e o sujeito é **he** → **has been**.

exercises.question: Qual frase está correta?
options: They have been waiting for hours when the show started.
They had been waiting for hours when the show started.
They has been waiting for hours when the show started.
They had been wait for hours when the show started.
exercises.explanation: "Started" é o evento passado de referência → **had been waiting**.

exercises.question: We ___ friends since childhood — nothing has changed.
exercises.explanation: A amizade começou no passado e **continua agora** → **have been**.
